t_ch: 你在这待着别动，我这就叫人送你出去。
t_en: You stay here and don't move. I'll get someone to escort you out.

t_ch: 现在的酒店里很危险，我让*罗斯玛丽*带你出去。
t_en: The hotel's dangerous right now. I'll have * Rosemary* escort you out.

t_ch: 你脑子里的雷达突然尖叫起来——跑、赶紧跑、跑得远远的，以免被这座即将爆发的火山波及。
t_en: Your mind's fight-or-flight instinct suddenly screams at you to run as fast as you can, or risk being swallowed by an erupting volcano.

t_ch: 烦死了、烦死了！
t_en: Agh! Enough! I'm tired of this!

t_ch: …我闻到了邪恶的味道…就在酒店深处——都给我消失吧！！！
t_en: ...I sense evil... deep inside the hotel... Away! Away, all of you!

t_ch: …滚开、都给我滚开……
t_en: ...Away, all of you...

t_ch: …你怎么了？我帮你叫…谁在那？！
t_en: ...Are you all right? I'll get some help for... Who's there!?
...Leave■ her... ■■She... destroyed■■■■■■... ■■■!!!

t_ch: …啊…没完没了…没完没了……
t_en: ...Ahh... It doesn't stop... It just doesn't stop...

t_ch: …刚刚…发生了什么…嘶——头好痛……
t_en: ...What happened... just now... Arggg— My head hurts...

t_ch: 但无论如何，是你帮了我吧？谢谢你。
t_en: Were you the one who helped me? Thank you.

t_ch: 准备好了就告诉我一声，我带你去安全的地方。
t_en: Let me know when you're ready. I'll take you to a safe place.

t_ch: 此刻的薇丝普依旧气势逼人，你却敏锐地发觉，这种无形的压迫力似乎*并不仅来源于她本人*。
t_en: Whisper remains as imposing as she was before, but the unseen force that you sense apparently is not *emanating from her*.

t_ch: 让你看笑话了…我知道你有很多问题。
t_en: Sorry, that must have been embarrassing... I know you must have many questions right now.

t_ch: 我从小就能看到常人看不见的东西。有人认为那是一种天赋…有人说我装神弄鬼，也有人说我有妄想症。
t_en: Since young, I've been able to see things that others can't. Some think it a gift... Others think I'm just fooling around, or that I'm delusional.

t_ch: 但无论别人怎么说，都不影响我每天睁眼闭眼都是黑影、鬼脸、各种可怕的怪物。
t_en: But no matter what they say, it still doesn't change the fact that I see shadows, horrifying visages and frightening monsters every waking and sleeping moment.

t_ch: 那些可怕的东西，哪怕是在我自己的梦中也会缠着我不放…我曾一度因为惧怕它们而不敢入眠。
t_en: Those horrible things even haunt me in my dreams... I was once so fearful of them that I didn't even want to fall asleep.

t_ch: 直到我来到匹诺康尼，并受邀加入了猎犬家系…他们看重我的天赋，认为我的「能力」能帮他们镇压暗藏于梦中的可怕存在。
t_en: That was until I came to Penacony and joined the Bloodhound Family... They valued my gift, and thought that my ability could help them suppress the terrifying existences that lurked in the dreams.

t_ch: 在猎犬的帮助下，我学会了和惊扰我的那些东西「共生」。你刚才…应该也遇到「她」了吧？
t_en: With their help, I learned how to co-exist with that which scared me. Just now... Did you see her as well?

t_ch: 总而言之，我现在已经习惯了这种生活。你也看到了，哪怕是「惊梦剧团」那样的敌人也不是我的对手。
t_en: In any case, I'm already used to this sort of life. As you've also seen, even the Dreamjolt Troupe is no match for me.

t_ch: 曾经困扰我的问题…现在成了我手里的武器。
t_en: The horrors that once used to trouble me... have now become my weapons.

t_ch: 一道锐利的目光刺穿漆黑的镜片，落在你身上。那目光中并无敌意，却仍令人胆颤心惊。
t_en: A sharp gaze pierces the jet-black lens, and Whisper's eyes descend on you. Her gaze is not hostile, but is frighteningly chilling nonetheless.

t_ch: 首先，对于阁下的帮助，我再次表示感谢。
t_en: First, I wish to thank you again for your aid.

t_ch: 但我还是需要警告您，关于我此前行为失控的诱因…我猜您自己也心知肚明。
t_en: But I still need to warn you about what caused me to lose control previously... I'm guessing you already know.

t_ch: 我不太清楚这其中的原理，我也愿意相信方才只是一场小小的意外…但我想提醒您，家族尽管尊重每一位逐梦客，但他们的忍耐也是有限度的。
t_en: I'm not sure of the actual reason, but I'm willing to believe what happened just now was simply a minor accident... Though, I still wish to remind you that even if The Family respects each and every dreamchaser, there is a limit to their patience.

t_ch: 为了匹诺康尼整体的和谐着想…希望您往后能够加倍慎重。
t_en: For the sake of Penacony's comprehensive harmony... I hope you will be more discreet moving forward.

t_ch: 首先，对于阁下的帮助，我再次表示感谢。
t_en: First, I wish to thank you again for your aid.

t_ch: 但我还是需要警告您，关于我此前行为失控的诱因…我猜您自己也心知肚明。
t_en: But I still need to warn you about what caused me to lose control previously... I'm guessing you already know.

t_ch: 我不太清楚这其中的原理，我也愿意相信方才只是一场小小的意外…但我想提醒您，家族尽管尊重每一位逐梦客，但他们的忍耐也是有限度的。
t_en: I'm not sure of the actual reason, but I'm willing to believe what happened just now was simply a minor accident... Though, I still wish to remind you that even if The Family respects each and every dreamchaser, there is a limit to their patience.

t_ch: 为了匹诺康尼整体的和谐着想…希望您往后能够加倍慎重。
t_en: For the sake of Penacony's comprehensive harmony... I hope you will be more discreet moving forward.

t_ch: 女人的表情变得柔和，嘴角浮现出一抹若有似无的笑意，让你不由好奇——
t_en: The woman's expression turns soft, an inscrutable hint of a smile flashing across the corners of her mouth — a most curious detail...

t_ch: …嗯？我刚刚笑了吗…没什么，只是想着*罗斯玛丽*应该快到了……
t_en: ...Hmm? Did I just smile... It's nothing. I was just thinking about *Rosemary's* impending arrival...

t_ch: 罗斯玛丽是怎样的人？
t_en: What kind of person is Rosemary?

t_ch: …罗斯玛丽啊…你要是想听的话，我倒是可以和你聊聊她。
t_en: ...Rosemary... If you wish to hear about her, I could tell you about her.

t_ch: 一直以来没什么人愿意同我讲话——从前大概是因为我太阴沉，而现在…人们在看我时，眼里总有种混合着恐惧的敬畏。
t_en: No one has ever agreed to chat with me. I suppose I was too gloomy before, but now... When people set their eyes on me, they see me with a mixture of fear and respect.

t_ch: 但罗斯玛丽…她可啰嗦了，平日里嘘寒问暖就算了，出任务的时候还总怕我磕着碰着…我又不是没长大的孩子…嘶——说来就来啊，你？
t_en: But Rosemary... She's such a chatterbox. It's fine if she did the regular platitudes, but she prattles during missions as well... It's not like I'm a child... Tsk... Ooh speak of the devil.

t_ch: …不是，我夸你呢…这不是说你体贴吗……
t_en: ...No, no. I was singing your praises... And how well you treat me...

t_ch: …我没受伤，真的！顶多就是衣服乱了点。
t_en: ...I'm not hurt, really! My clothes are ruffled, is all.

t_ch: …好吧，是我干的，不过真没受伤。你还不知道吗？那些惊梦剧团见了我，跑都来不及。
t_en: ...All right. I did it, but I'm really not hurt. Don't you get it? Those Dreamjolt Troupe cowards turn tail at the sight of me.

t_ch: …{F#她}{M#他}？哦，{F#她}{M#他}是被困在酒店里的宾客，我正打算送{F#她}{M#他}出去……
t_en: ...{F#Her}{M#Him}? Oh, {F#She's}{M#He's} a guest who's trapped in the hotel. I was just about to escort {F#her}{M#him} out...

t_ch: 你现在要离开酒店吗？
t_en: Are you leaving the hotel now?

t_ch: 好啊，辛苦你了。
t_en: Sure, thank you very much.

t_ch: 罗斯玛丽，可以麻烦你帮忙带{F#她}{M#他}出去吗？拜托啦……
t_en: Rosemary, could I trouble you to lead {F#her}{M#him} outside? Thank you...

t_ch: 先不了，我还有事。
t_en: It's fine. I've got something else on.

t_ch: 我知道了。如果有需要，随时来找我。
t_en: Got it. Let me know if you need something.

t_ch: 嗯…你怎么又回来了？即便你有能力自保，也还是尽量少在这种地方闲逛为好。
t_en: Hmm... What're you doing back here? Even if you're strong, you shouldn't loiter too much in a place like this.

t_ch: 需要送你离开酒店吗？
t_en: Do you need me to send you out of the hotel?

t_ch: 好啊，辛苦你了。
t_en: Sure, thank you very much.

t_ch: 罗斯玛丽，可以麻烦你再帮个忙吗？
t_en: Rosemary, can you help me again?

t_ch: 先不了，我还有事。
t_en: It's fine. I've got something else on.

t_ch: 我知道了。如果有需要，随时来找我。
t_en: Got it. Let me know if you need something.

t_ch: 我也只是个普通人啊……
t_en: I'm also just a normal person...

t_ch: 能够理解我、陪伴我的，就只有罗斯玛丽一个……
t_en: The only one who understands and stays by my side is Rosemary...

t_ch: 我也只是个普通人…每天面对那些奇形怪状的玩意，这么久还没疯已经很不容易了。
t_en: I'm also just a normal person... It's miracle I haven't gone insane, seeing those grotesque things everyday.

t_ch: 从前…也有人关心过我。但那更像是一种同情，一种类似于「看到有只狗掉进了湖里」的怜悯……
t_en: Before... There used to be people who cared for me, but it was more out of compassion, a kind of sympathy you felt when you saw a dog fall down a well...

t_ch: …只有罗斯玛丽，一直以来是她教导我如何克服恐惧，让我成长为如今的样子。
t_en: ...Not Rosemary though. She's the one who taught me how to face my fears, allowing me to grow into the person I am today.

t_ch: 我们之间有种奇妙的联系。我会因她的快乐而喜悦，她也会因我的悲伤而哭泣。
t_en: We share a wondrous relationship: I will be joyful because she's happy, and she will cry because of my sadness.

t_ch: 能够来到匹诺康尼、能够在梦里遇见她——这或许是我此生最大的幸运了。
t_en: To be able to meet her in a dream on Penacony ... This might be the most fortunate thing to happen to me.

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: …很少有人会想要和我聊天。
t_en: ...Few people wish to speak with me.

t_ch: 问吧，接你的人来之前，我都在这里。
t_en: Ask away. I'll be here before your escort arrives.

t_ch: 酒店到底发生了什么？
t_en: What exactly happened at the hotel?

t_ch: …酒店所在梦境突然发生大规模坍塌，原因尚未查明。
t_en: ...A large swathe of the Dreamscape the hotel was in suddenly started collapsing, but the reasons are not yet known.

t_ch: 我们接到命令，组织内部人员撤离，并将其全面封锁。
t_en: We received orders to rally the staff and evacuate everyone, then lock down the entire place.

t_ch: 你…不用担心，猎犬会处理好一切。
t_en: You... don't have to worry. The Hounds will take care of it.

t_ch: 你是这里的负责人？
t_en: Are you in charge of this place?

t_ch: 放心，在我身边，你绝对安全。
t_en: Relax. You're absolutely safe by my side.

t_ch: 罗斯玛丽是谁？
t_en: Who's Rosemary?

t_ch: …我的朋友，也是最可靠的工作伙伴。
t_en: ...A friend of mine, and the most trusty work partner I can have.

t_ch: 没什么问题了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 嗯。
t_en: Yeah.

t_ch: 你叫的人还没来吗？
t_en: Is the person you called for not here yet?

t_ch: 奇怪，信号好像出了点问题…你切勿随意走动，我先联系*罗斯玛丽*带你出去。
t_en: Strange. Something's wrong with the signal... Please don't wander. I'll contact *Rosemary* to bring you out.

t_ch: 你是……
t_en: ...And you are?

t_ch: 我不记得公馆近期有安排贵宾参观的计划，这到底——
t_en: I don't remember seeing any VIP guests scheduled to visit. What's all this...

t_ch: 男子打断了说到一半的语句。他微微收起下颌，镜片反射出的光亮将他的眉眼藏匿了起来。
t_en: He cuts himself off mid-sentence, slightly lowering his chin so that the light reflecting off his glasses masks his eyes.

t_ch: …啊，我知晓了。既然如此，我可以默许你在朝露公馆活动，只要你不做出任何出格的行为。
t_en: ...Ah, I see. In that case, I'll give tacit consent for anything you want to do here in Dewlight Pavilion, so long as it's nothing out of the ordinary.

t_ch: 我很乖的。
t_en: I'll be on my best behavior.

t_ch: 是吗？那和我得到的消息之间多少有些…偏差。不重要了。
t_en: Really? There's a slight... discrepancy between that statement and what I've been told. It's no matter, though.

t_ch: 自我介绍一下——我是麦考。家主外出期间，我就是这间公馆的代理人。
t_en: Allow me to introduce myself: I'm McCoy. I'm responsible for this pavilion while the family head is away.

t_ch: 你算老几？
t_en: So, how high up the totem pole are you?

t_ch: 出乎意料…意图连结银河的开拓客，发言竟会如此粗鄙。回答你的问题——我是麦考。家主外出期间，我就是这间公馆的代理。
t_en: My word... Who knew Trailblazers hoping to unite the entire cosmos could be so uncouth? To answer your question: I'm McCoy, and I'm responsible for this pavilion while the family head is away.

t_ch: 遵照入梦守则，擅闯「朝露的时刻」的逐梦客会即刻被驱逐出梦，并终身不得再入住「白日梦」酒店。这次，我算是特别为你们破了个例。
t_en: Based on the Dreaming Regulations, Dreamchasers who trespass the Moment of Morning Dew shall be instantly expelled from the Dreamscapes and banned for life from ever staying at The Reverie Hotel. This time, I've elected to make a special exception for you.

t_ch: 毕竟，诸位此前为家族的调查贡献了不少力气。
t_en: After all, you have hitherto contributed much toward The Family's investigation.

t_ch: 你是怎么知道的？
t_en: How did you know?

t_ch: 啊…我还以为对于坐拥智库的星穹列车而言，这类知识等同于常识。
t_en: Ah... I assumed this was common knowledge with all the information the Astral Express possesses.

t_ch: 避免长篇大论地说，当一位家族成员在「同谐」的路上走得足够远…他们便能获得心灵相通的力量，不必通过面会口传亦能获知彼此的见闻。
t_en: To keep things brief: When a member of The Family walks far enough on the Path of Harmony... they gain the power of tacit understanding, allowing information to be exchanged without the need for face-to-face communication.

t_ch: 所以，我知道诸位经历了什么。或许不是全部的细节…但仅凭那些片段也足够我做出判断。
t_en: So, I know all that you have been through. Perhaps not all of the details, but... enough for me to arrive at my own conclusions.

t_ch: 但我还是奉劝各位，在梦境之地尽量恪守家族制定的规则。至于原因，我刚才已经解释过了——家族时刻都在看着你。
t_en: However, I still advise you all to abide by the laws as set out by The Family while in the Dreamscape. As for the reason why, I've just demonstrated my explanation: The Family is always watching you.

t_ch: 鉴于诸位的身份和过去的行为，我破例准许你们在朝露公馆自由行动。
t_en: In light of your status and previous behavior, I am making an exception and allowing you free rein in Dewlight Pavilion.

t_ch: 但我还是奉劝各位，在梦境之地尽量恪守家族制定的规则——记住，家族时刻都在看着你。
t_en: However, I still advise you all while in the Dreamscape to abide by the laws as set out by The Family. Remember, The Family is always watching you.

t_ch: 在你释放完钟表把戏的瞬间，麦考先生的脸上闪过了一连串的神情——从困惑到扭曲，从扭曲到痛苦…最后定格在了悲伤之上。
t_en: The moment you finish releasing the clockwork, a series of expressions flash over Mr. McCoy's face: From confusion to a grimace and then to pain... before finally settling on a look of sadness.

t_ch: 你…你做了什么？
t_en: What... what did you do?

t_ch: 我…不对，我不该想起来的…我明明已经忘记了……
t_en: I... no, I shouldn't be able to remember... I've undoubtedly forgotten...

t_ch: 你的情绪和记忆被人偷走了。
t_en: Your emotions and memories have been stolen.

t_ch: …偷走？不对，那不是偷盗行为。是我…是我自愿上缴了我的「悲伤」…还有那些让我痛苦的回忆……
t_en: ...Stolen? No, that's not stealing. I... I voluntarily handed over my "sadness"... and all of those painful memories...

t_ch: 忘记了什么？
t_en: Forgotten something?

t_ch: 我想起来了…是我…是我自愿上缴了我的「悲伤」…还有那些让我痛苦的回忆……
t_en: I remember... I... I voluntarily handed over my "sadness"... and all of those painful memories...

t_ch: 为什么…为什么你要让我记起来？谁给了你…这个资格？
t_en: Why... why do you want to make me remember? Who gave you this... this power?

t_ch: 偷走情绪才是恶劣的行为。
t_en: Stealing emotions is evil.

t_ch: 人总得面对生命中那些破事。
t_en: We all have to face down bad things in life.

t_ch: 你究竟经历了什么？
t_en: What happened to you?

t_ch: 我…一直在后悔。我以为那么做——放弃那些记忆——会让我过得好受些…但我却变得更痛苦了。
t_en: I... I've always regretted it. I thought doing that — abandoning those memories — would make me feel better... but all it did was make me even sadder.

t_ch: 折磨我的噩梦没有消失…而我甚至忘记了自己究竟为什么会感到悔恨。
t_en: The nightmares that tortured me, they never went away... and I even began to forget why exactly I felt such regret.

t_ch: 麦考先生从大衣的口袋中掏出了一张照片……
t_en: Mr. McCoy fishes a photo from out of his breast pocket...

t_ch: 原来这就是我的痛苦源泉。我现在全都记起来了…我再也不用每天盯着这张照片，却找不到心脏为之阵痛的原因了。
t_en: It turned out that this here was the source of all my pain. I can remember it all, now... No longer shall I stare into this photo without knowing the reason behind why my heart is broken.

t_ch: 我需要一些帮助…来补救我的过去。
t_en: I need some help... reconciling my past.

t_ch: 帮人忙我最擅长。
t_en: I'm the best there is at helping people out.

t_ch: 说说看你需要什么。
t_en: Tell me what it is you need.

t_ch: 难道…你愿意帮助我吗？没有任何理由，只是出于同情？
t_en: Are you really... going to help me? No strings attached, purely just out of sympathy?

t_ch: 我…为之前表现出来的冷漠道歉。或许他们说的是对的，你们这些开拓客总能给人带去希望。
t_en: I... apologize for the indifference before. Perhaps they are right and you Trailblazers really do always bring hope to others.

t_ch: 可以的话，请你拿上这张照片——然后去这公馆的别处找到我的<color=#dbc291ff>兄弟姐妹</color>。他们应该…很显眼。
t_en: If it's okay, please could you hold onto this photo — and then go and find my <color=#dbc291ff>brother and sister</color> elsewhere in this pavilion. They should... be quite conspicuous.

t_ch: 我没有勇气去面对他们…所以我需要你的帮助。等你把他们带回来，我会把所有事情和你解释清楚的。
t_en: I haven't the courage to face them myself... so I need your help. I'll explain everything to you once you bring them back.

t_ch: 可以的话，请你拿上这张照片——然后去这公馆的别处找到我的<color=#dbc291ff>兄弟姐妹</color>。他们应该…很显眼。
t_en: If it's okay, please could you hold onto this photo — and then go and find my <color=#dbc291ff>brother and sister</color> elsewhere in this pavilion. They should... be quite conspicuous.

t_ch: 我没有勇气去面对他们…所以我需要你的帮助。等你<color=#dbc291ff>把他们带回来</color>，我会把事情的来龙去脉和你解释清楚的。
t_en: I haven't the courage to face them myself... so I need your help. I'll tell you the whole story <color=#dbc291ff>once you bring them back</color>.

t_ch: 麦考…麦考……
t_en: McCoy... McCoy...

t_ch: （这个家伙…好像一直在念叨着麦考先生的名字……）
t_en: (This guy... sounds like he's mumbling McCoy's name over and over...)

t_ch: （麦考先生说过，要我<color=#dbc291ff>把他的兄弟姐妹们给带回去</color>…可是到底该怎么做？）
t_en: (Mr. McCoy said he wants me to <color=#dbc291ff>bring back his brother and sister</color>... but how?)

t_ch: 麦考…麦考……
t_en: McCoy... McCoy...

t_ch: （试着和它对话一下吧……）
t_en: (Try talking with it...)

t_ch: 呃…你好？
t_en: Er... Hello?

t_ch: 介意和我走一趟吗？
t_en: Would you mind taking a stroll with me?

t_ch: 偿还……
t_en: Payback...

t_ch: （偿还…它在说什么？）
t_en: (Payback? What's it talking about?)

t_ch: （感觉有些不妙…这家伙好像有些生气了。）
t_en: (Doesn't sound very good... This guy seems kinda angry.)

t_ch: 偿还…偿还……
t_en: Payback... payback...

t_ch: （糟了，准备迎战！）
t_en: (Rats. Time to get ready for a fight!)

t_ch: <size=28>偿还…偿还！！！</size>
t_en: <size=28>Payback... payback!!</size>

t_ch: （呼…没想到还是要这么收场。还以为那家伙没什么攻击性……）
t_en: (Phew... Never thought things would wrap up like that. I didn't take that guy to be so aggressive...)

t_ch: 随着「弟弟」的消散，一阵暖意钻入了你外套的口袋中…那是你存放麦考先生的照片的地方。
t_en: As the "Little Brother" disappears, you feel a warm jet of something streaming into your coat pocket... right where you stashed the photo from Mr. McCoy.
(Huh? This is...)

t_ch: （奇怪，照片上…多出来了一个人？）
t_en: (That's weird. Is that... another person in the photo now?)

t_ch: （刚才的那个怪物…难道它就是麦考先生的兄弟吗？）
t_en: (That strange creature just then... Don't tell me it was Mr. McCoy's brother?)

t_ch: （麦考先生好像还提到了他的「姐妹」…去公馆的别处找找看吧。）
t_en: (Mr. McCoy also mentioned his "sister"... Time to check some other places around the pavilion.)

t_ch: 麦考…麦考……
t_en: McCoy... McCoy...

t_ch: （这个「东西」…大概就是麦考先生的姐妹吧？）
t_en: (This "thing"... is probably going to be Mr. McCoy's sister, right?)

t_ch: （得保持警觉…说不定她也会突然跳起来攻击我。）
t_en: (Gotta stay alert... she might just leap up too and attack me out of nowhere.)

t_ch: 麦考…麦考……
t_en: McCoy... McCoy...

t_ch: 想吃…想吃……
t_en: Wanna eat... Wanna eat...

t_ch: （她是不是在说…她想吃东西？迷因怪物也会有饥饿感吗？）
t_en: (Is she saying... she wants to eat something? Can meme monsters feel hunger, too?)

t_ch: 酸酸的…甜甜的……
t_en: Sour... sweet...

t_ch: （看起来她好像没有什么攻击性…要不要给她一些她想要的东西呢？）
t_en: (She doesn't seem to be that aggressive... Why don't we give her a little of what she wants?)

t_ch: 眼前的忆域迷因轻轻地接过了你手中的软糖，然后背过身去……
t_en: This Memory Zone Meme gently takes the soft candy from your hand and then turns away from you...

t_ch: 「姐姐」发出了嘎吱、嘎吱的咀嚼声…然后陷入了沉默。
t_en: The "Big Sister" lets out a few gnashing and gnawing sounds... before falling dead silent.

t_ch: 几秒之后，你好像听到了一些微弱的声音…仿佛是有人在悄悄流泪啜泣。
t_en: A few seconds later, you hear another ever-so-faint sound... as though someone were quietly sobbing.

t_ch: 甜甜的…酸酸的……
t_en: Sweet... sour...

t_ch: 小时候…小时候……
t_en: When I was young... when I was young...

t_ch: 想回去…想回去……
t_en: Wanna go back... wanna go back...

t_ch: 「姐姐」在你面前消散了。一阵暖意随之钻入了你外套的口袋中…那是你存放麦考先生和他弟弟的照片的地方。
t_en: The "Big Sister" disappears right in front of your eyes, and you feel a warm jet of something streaming into your coat pocket... right where you stashed the photo of Mr. McCoy and his brother.

t_ch: （照片上又多了一个人……）
t_en: (There's now another person in the photo...)

t_ch: （虽然还不知道究竟发生了什么…但这样麦考先生的委托应该就算完成了吧？）
t_en: (Despite still having no idea of what's going on... that should be it for Mr. McCoy's assignment, right?)

t_ch: （该回去找他问个究竟了。）
t_en: (Let's go back and ask him about what happened.)

t_ch: 你好，开拓客。
t_en: Hello there, Trailblazer.

t_ch: 之前的那件事…进展如何？
t_en: How are things... with that matter from before?

t_ch: 已经完成了。
t_en: All done and dusted.

t_ch: 你将那张不再是麦考先生孑然一身的照片递给了他——
t_en: Just as you are about to hand over the photo to Mr. McCoy — who's no longer alone in the picture...

t_ch: ——在那之前，你又最后瞥了一眼那张照片。奇怪的是，照片上的三个人似乎都露出了一丝笑容…温暖得有些不真实。
t_en: ...you steal one last fleeting glance at the photo. What's strange is that all three people in the photo are flashing smiles... so warm that they seem a little unnatural.
Ah...

t_ch: 你真的办到了…谢谢你。
t_en: You actually did it... Thank you.

t_ch: 这样…我的痛苦大概就能缓和些了。虽然这改变不了什么…但多多少少算是一点心灵上的安慰。
t_en: This is how... I can ease the pain a little. Even though it doesn't change a thing... it still gives me a little bit of spiritual comfort.

t_ch: 该告诉我这到底是怎么回事了。
t_en: Time to tell me what exactly is going on.

t_ch: 当然，毕竟我们之前已经说好了。
t_en: Of course, as I had promised.

t_ch: 你应该已经见过我的兄弟姐妹了…你应该也在迷惑他们为什么会变成那副模样，对吧？
t_en: You should have met my brother and sister by now... and you must be confused about why they look like that, yes?

t_ch: …那是我造成的。我就是害他们变成那种怪物的凶手。
t_en: ...It's all my fault. I was the one that caused them to become such monsters.

t_ch: 你是怎么做到的？
t_en: How did it happen?

t_ch: …原来你好奇的是把梦中的人转化成迷因的方法吗？
t_en: ...So you want to know more about how people are turned into memes in their dreams?

t_ch: 为什么要这么做？
t_en: Why did you do this?

t_ch: 照片上的两个人分别是我的弟弟，约翰；还有我的姐姐，詹妮弗。
t_en: These two in the photo are my little brother John and my sister Jennifer.

t_ch: 我们都是匹诺康尼本地人——成年之后，我们都经过了「同谐」的洗礼，成为了家族的一员。
t_en: We were all Penacony locals... and all baptized into Harmony when we came of age, becoming members of The Family.

t_ch: 詹妮弗是个天资过人的舞者，她不费什么力气就成为了鸢尾花的一员。约翰…他的经历没那么顺利，但最后也成功当上了猎犬。
t_en: Jennifer was a gifted dancer who managed to join up with the Iris Family without needing to try very hard. As for John... his journey wasn't so smooth but he still ended up becoming one of the Bloodhounds.

t_ch: 我本以为，我们的人生就会这样一直顺利下去——我们兄弟姐妹三人，走在神主铺好的道路上，一起用自己的力量为美梦添砖加瓦，直到世界的尽头……
t_en: At first, I thought our lives would go on perfectly like this... We three siblings, walking the paths set out for us by the Great One, each using our strengths to do our bit and build up the Dreamscapes until the end of time...

t_ch: 但是…
t_en: But then...

t_ch: …毫不意外地出了意外。
t_en: ...Of course, something unexpected just had to happen.

t_ch: 我还记得那一天…那时我还只是一个小小的酒店管家，还从未踏入过这间公馆。那一天，家主毫无征兆地来到了我的面前。
t_en: I can still remember the day... Back then, I was just a lowly hotel butler who'd never set foot in this pavilion. That day, the family head came and approached me with no notice or warning.

t_ch: 我还记得自己当时的心情。我惶恐无比…也激动无比。我记得家主轻轻拍了拍我的肩膀，然后向我提出了那个要求——那个让我的大脑彻底一片空白的要求。
t_en: I still remember how I felt that day. Absolutely terrified... and absolutely thrilled. I remember the family head gently tapping me on the shoulder and making this request of me... a request that made my mind go completely blank.

t_ch: 他让我——约翰的哥哥，詹妮弗的弟弟——「抹消他们的存在」。因为他们是「家族的叛徒」…因为他们试图将家族的「信息」出卖给公司。
t_en: He asked me — the elder brother of John's and the younger brother of Jennifer's... to "wipe away their very existences" because they were "traitors to The Family"... because they'd been trying to sell off Family "information" to the IPC.

t_ch: 然后…你照做了？
t_en: And so... so then, you did it?

t_ch: …对，因为我没有选择。下达那个命令的甚至并非橡木的家主…而是在他之上的梦主本人。
t_en: ...Yes, because I had no other choice. That order didn't even come from the Oak Family head... but rather from their overlord, the Dreammaster.

t_ch: 我用…「家族的方式」，抹消了约翰和詹妮弗在梦中的存在——一劳永逸，不留痕迹。
t_en: I used... "The Family Way"... to wipe out the very existence of John and Jennifer from the dreamscape once and for all, without a trace.

t_ch: 回想起来，我下手时竟然没有太多迟疑。或许是因为除了悲痛，我更多地是感到了愤怒和羞辱吧？
t_en: Looking back, I barely resisted when it came time to act. Maybe it's because of the anger and the humiliation that I felt, besides all the grief?

t_ch: 我感觉自己受到了背叛…因为我无法理解他们怎么能背着我做出那种行径。我们是同父同母的兄弟姐妹…我们本应无话不说，没有隔阂，像真正的「家人」那样。
t_en: I felt betrayed... because I couldn't understand how they could do that behind my back. We're siblings from the same mother and father... there should be nothing that we keep from each other. No barriers between us. Just like a real "family."

t_ch: 我的忠诚得到了回报。家主将我带到了朝露公馆，听说了我的…「事迹」的人，全都对我敬畏三分。
t_en: My loyalty ended up paying off though. The family head brought me to Dewlight Pavilion and everyone who heard about my... "deeds" was in awe of what I'd done.

t_ch: 但不久之后，可怕的梦魇开始追逐我，折磨我，令我一度畏惧入梦。为了摆脱那些面容可怖的影子…我主动上缴了自己的记忆。我选择了忘记。
t_en: But not long after, I became plagued by haunting nightmares that tortured me. I became too scared to fall asleep. To rid myself of those harrowing shadows, I... I handed over control of my own memories. I chose to forget everything.

t_ch: 但你永远都不会忘记。
t_en: But you could never forget.

t_ch: 当然不会…当然不会。
t_en: Of course I couldn't... of course not.

t_ch: 这是你应得的报应。
t_en: You got what you deserved.

t_ch: 或许吧…或许吧。
t_en: Perhaps... perhaps.

t_ch: 但至少你暂时为我安抚了我的兄弟姐妹…解放了他们残余的怨念化成的迷因。
t_en: But at least you've offered temporary solace to my brother and sister... releasing the animosity lingering in those memes.

t_ch: 现实中的他们还在沉睡，一直都没有醒来。或许等到哪一天，等到梦主认为他们已经赎清了罪过…我们还能再次相遇，相拥……
t_en: In reality, they're still asleep in a never-ending slumber. Perhaps one day, when the Dreammaster considers their sins as being redeemed... perhaps we'll be able to meet again and hold one another...

t_ch: …我今天已经吐露了太多秘密了，但愿短期内不会有人深掘我的意识。感谢你的帮助，开拓客…拿去吧，这些是你应得的奖赏。
t_en: ...I've already let enough secrets out of the bag for today. Hopefully, nobody takes a deep dive into my consciousness for the time being. Thank you for your help, Trailblazer... And take this, as it's the least you deserve.

t_ch: 想和你打听些事。
t_en: I'd like to ask you something.

t_ch: 说吧，但愿不是无关紧要的提问。
t_en: Go ahead. I hope it's nothing trivial.

t_ch: 你是哪个家系的？
t_en: Which family do you represent?

t_ch: 橡木。
t_en: The Oaks.

t_ch: 我已经回答完了。还有别的事吗？
t_en: I've answered all your questions. Anything else?

t_ch: 这间公馆一直这么冷清吗？
t_en: Has this pavilion always been so deserted?

t_ch: 理应如此——这间公馆是为了供五大家主合议匹诺康尼的重大事宜才设立的。「黄金的时刻」里的那番景象并不是梦境的全部，我相信你能明白这个道理。
t_en: I should hope so — it was built for the five major family heads to collectively discuss any matter critical to Penacony. The scene in Golden Hour isn't all there is to the Dreamscapes. I'm sure you get what I mean.

t_ch: 每场令宾客满意的盛会，都是周密的筹备和编排的结果。如此说来，这间「冷清」的公馆才是一切令人陶醉的美梦的起点。
t_en: Each and every event put on to please our guests is painstakingly prepared and arranged. That's why this "deserted" pavilion is actually the springboard for all the mesmerizing dreams here.

t_ch: 你这人总是这么严肃吗？
t_en: Have you always been this serious?

t_ch: 这里不是「白日梦」酒店，我也不是接待宾客的服务人员。
t_en: This isn't The Reverie Hotel, and I'm no front-of-house receptionist.

t_ch: 我的职责是在家主外出期间打理公馆的事务…我不需要用笑脸应对一切事态和突发状况，特别是不期而至的访客。
t_en: My job is to handle affairs when the family head is absent... I'm not required to keep a smile handy for every occasion or emergent situation — least of all for unexpected guests.

t_ch: 没什么事了。
t_en: Nothing much.

t_ch: 记住，谨言慎行。
t_en: Remember, be careful with everything you say and do.

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 记住，谨言慎行。
t_en: Remember, be careful with everything you say and do.

t_ch: 我的过去，一直纠缠着我。
t_en: My past has always haunted me.

t_ch: 但我宁愿永远背负这份痛苦，也不想再选择逃避。
t_en: But I choose to shoulder this pain, I will not run away anymore.

t_ch: 麦考先生的眼角垂了下来…你知道，他一定又开始思念自己的兄弟姐妹了。
t_en: Mr. McCoy's eyes grew sullen... You know that he must be thinking about his siblings again.

t_ch: 到底是梦主的威压太过恐怖…还是我本就是个冷血无情的魔鬼？
t_en: Was the Dreammaster really that terrifying and intimidating... Or am I just a cold-blooded demon?

t_ch: 我已经分不清了。我只知道自己在下手的时候并没有太多犹豫。
t_en: I can't even tell anymore. I just know that when I made my move, I did not hesitate for long.

t_ch: 我得到了自己一直想要的东西：财富，权力，地位。我付出的代价？两个给我带来痛苦的影子，还有一个挥之不去的噩梦。
t_en: I received everything I've ever wanted: Money, power, status. The price I paid? Two shadows that bring constant pain and a nightmare that I cannot escape.

t_ch: 朝露公馆是家族重地，外人不宜久留。
t_en: Dewlight Pavilion is a Family stronghold, it is not a safe place for outsiders to remain for too long.

t_ch: 这是善意的提醒，不是威胁……
t_en: This is just a polite warning, it is not a threat...

t_ch: 麦考先生微微后仰，眼镜反射出的光芒又一次遮蔽了他的双眼。
t_en: Mr. McCoy tilted his head back slightly, as the glare from his glasses obscured his eyes once more.

t_ch: 作为朝露公馆的代理执行者，我有义务提醒您：任何企图剽窃或破坏家族资产的行为都有可能导致严重的后果。
t_en: As the acting executor of Dewlight Pavilion, I am obliged to inform you that any attempts to plagiarize or destroy Family assets may result in grave consequences.

t_ch: 希望您能保持理智，不要做出任何出格的行为……
t_en: I hope that you can remain rational and refrain from any improper actions...

t_ch: 麦考先生没有把话说死——很明显，他知道你曾在梦中做过哪些丢人的事……
t_en: Mr. McCoy was not specific with his phrasing... He clearly knows the shameful things that you have done in the Dreamscape...

t_ch: 「绿色的小鸟，衔着脆嫩的枝芽，在暖阳之下筑巢……」
t_en: "Little green bird with the flower bud, building a nest beneath the sun..."

t_ch: 麦考先生陷入一阵甜蜜的回忆，似乎无法自拔。
t_en: Mr. McCoy has fallen deep into a sweet memory. He seems unable to pull himself back from it.

t_ch: 麦考先生的嘴角露出一丝笑意——对于像他这样面若冰霜的男人来说，嘴角那丝细微的角度变化看来竟如此不可思议。
t_en: As he reminisces, a faint smile grows at the corner of his mouth... For an icy man like Mr. McCoy, even this slight change of expression seems incomprehensible.

t_ch: 啊…我突然想起一段美好的时光。
t_en: Ah... I just recalled a wonderful time.

t_ch: 我们兄弟姐妹三人，躺在一片被暖阳染成金黄色的草坪上…姐姐哼唱着小时最熟悉的歌谣，约翰在一边把玩一根不知从何处捡来的树枝……
t_en: We three siblings were lying on a mound of grass that had been dyed a light yellow by the warm sun... My sister was singing a nursery rhyme from our childhood, as John was off playing with a branch he picked up somewhere...

t_ch: 我把手伸向天空，一只绿色的小鸟落在我的指尖。那缕温暖的阳光宛若松脂，它凝结了时光，也定格了我们三人的笑容……
t_en: I reached my hand out toward the sky and a small, green bird landed on my fingertip. That warm sunshine was like golden resin, preserving us at that moment and fixing the smiles on our faces...

t_ch: 麦考先生陷入一阵甜蜜的回忆，似乎无法自拔。
t_en: Mr. McCoy has fallen deep into a sweet memory. He seems unable to pull himself back from it.

t_ch: 这世上存在诸多罪孽，其中唯有贪婪和背叛最不可原谅。
t_en: There are many sins in this world, among them, greed and betrayal stand as the most unforgivable.

t_ch: 很遗憾…我的兄弟姐妹并不懂得这个道理。
t_en: Unfortunately... my brother and sister did not understand this.

t_ch: 麦考先生双眉颦蹙，你成功激起了他内心的不快。
t_en: Mr. McCoy's brows grew furrowed, you have successfully brought his sadness to the surface.

t_ch: 所谓万人之上，五人之下……
t_en: Though I stand above the many, there are still five that stand above me...

t_ch: 为了爬升家族的音阶，我已经倾尽了一切手段，甚至把我的兄弟姐妹都当成了晋升路上的垫脚石。
t_en: I have used every method at my disposal to climb up The Family's scales, even using my own siblings as footholds on my path to ascension.

t_ch: 我距离那五人究竟还有多远…到底还要付出怎样的代价，我才能跻身家主的行列？
t_en: Just how much further away am I from those final five... Just what kind of price do I have to pay to join the ranks of family heads?

t_ch: 他们颐指气使的态度令我恼火。总有一天，我会……
t_en: I can no longer stand their arrogant and domineering attitudes. One day, I will...

t_ch: 麦考先生压制住了怒气，但他的野心早已在你眼前展露无遗。
t_en: Mr. McCoy managed to suppress his rage, but his ambition is now wholly clear to you.

t_ch: 感谢你的帮助，{F#小姐}{M#年轻人}…但我还是想提醒你，朝露公馆是家族重地，像你这样的外人不宜久留。
t_en: Thank you for your help, {F#young lady}{M#young man}... However, I must remind you that Dewlight Pavilion is a Family stronghold, it is not wise for outsiders like yourself to remain for too long.

t_ch: 我还记得上一个试图私闯公馆的逐梦客。他的下场…算了，你不会想知道的。
t_en: I still remember the last Dreamchaser to try and infiltrate the pavilion. He ended up... No, you would not want to know.

t_ch: 把你该做的事做完，然后尽快离开吧。
t_en: Please take care of your affairs and leave as quickly as possible.

t_ch: 孩子，有兴趣了解下银河系战区救助基金会吗？
t_en: Hey, kid, would you like to learn about the Galactic Warzone Relief Foundation?

t_ch: 基金会致力于消除战争带来的苦难，迄今已有数家跨星系企业加盟，为共八十亿以上的难民提供了医疗、食品、就业等人道主义援助。
t_en: The foundation is dedicated to eliminating the suffering caused by war, with several intergalactic enterprises joining forces to provide medicine, food, employment, and other forms of humanitarian assistance for over eight billion refugees.

t_ch: 可我不是企业家。
t_en: I'm no philanthropist.

t_ch: 孩子，在企业家外，基金会内更多也是无数个像你一样心地善良的人。
t_en: Kid, in addition to scions of business there are also countless numbers of kind-hearted souls just like you who make up the foundation.

t_ch: 时刻心怀善意，即使你并不富裕，撒下的种子也会在未来开花结果。只要有人因基金会的呼吁而心生良善，我们的努力就成功了。
t_en: Always harbor good intentions, even if you may not be rich — For one day, the seeds you sow will bear fruit in the future. So long as the foundation's cries fall on kind ears, our endeavors will count as a success.

t_ch: 我也来帮帮场子。
t_en: Let me help out too.

t_ch: 感谢你的心意，孩子。
t_en: Bless your kindness, kid.

t_ch: 正因为有许许多多同你一样善良的朋友，基金会才能运营至今。金钱只是慈善事业的血管，发自内心的善意才是供血的心脏。
t_en: It's precisely because of so many kind-hearted friends such as yourself that the foundation is able to carry on to this day. Money merely forms the arteries of any charity, but it's kindness from within that pumps the lifeblood like a heart.

t_ch: 这个宇宙充满了罪恶。孩子无家可归，奴隶被套上项圈，人不再像人，死亡成为冰冷的数字，发动战争的刽子手却对此沾沾自喜。
t_en: This universe is filled with sin. Orphans with no place to call home, slaves bound in shackles, people deprived of their own humanity... The dead are nothing but a cold number beneath the smug hand of the executioners who commissioned these wars.

t_ch: 纵使无法阻止战争的发生，但至少能减轻它带来的创伤，让财富发挥它真正的作用。
t_en: Even if we have no way of stopping war from happening, we can at least mitigate the trauma caused by letting wealth play out its true purpose.

t_ch: 这就是我组建基金会的初衷。
t_en: That's the original idea behind establishing this foundation of mine.

t_ch: 愿世界和平。
t_en: May peace come to all worlds.

t_ch: 望战争不再。
t_en: May war come nevermore.

t_ch: 放宽心，孩子。会实现的，总有一天，我打心里这样祈盼。到那时，基金会便没了存在的必要，我的心也能重拾真正的欢欣和喜悦。
t_en: Relax, kid. It will become true. One day it will — that I pray, from the bottom of my heart. And when that day comes, the foundation will lose its raison d'être — and I'll be free to pick up my true passion and joy once more.

t_ch: 于是大家笑着，手牵着手，不分种族，也不分贵贱。
t_en: And so everyone will come to laugh again and hold hands together, indiscriminate of species or class.

t_ch: 到那时，我就把公司交给别人，一个人找颗安静的星球。躺在草原上，什么也不想，什么也不做。
t_en: When that day comes, I'm going to hand off my business to someone else, find a quiet planet somewhere all to myself, and just lie on the grass — thinking about nothing and doing even less.

t_ch: 你做的很好，孩子，保持这份善意。
t_en: You're doing great, kid. Keep on being kind.

t_ch: 奥科萨娜那布道者般的浅笑突然咧开，把你吓了一跳。她试图收敛自己的笑意，可似乎不太成功。
t_en: Oksana's muted saintly smile suddenly breaks out into a grin, startling you. She tries taking it back, but it doesn't seem to work.

t_ch: 我的心脏在苏乐达里跳舞，钟表小子和洋娃娃手牵手，大把大把的账单滚滚流。
t_en: My heart is dancing in SoulGlad. Clockie and the doll are holding hands, and the skies are showering with money.

t_ch: 我的快乐就像唱歌的红巨星，在真空里缓慢膨胀，变成数字增长。
t_en: My happiness is like a red giant star singing as it slowly expands in the vacuum of space, shooting high like a growing number.

t_ch: 嘿，孩子，你知道吗？在我们的星球，人们在星夜下一边起舞一边洽谈生意，你的舞步越是出众，生意也越是兴隆。
t_en: Hey kid, y'know what? On my home planet, people dance beneath the starry night sky while we do business deals. The more outrageous your dance moves are, the more lucrative your deal is going to be.

t_ch: 离开家乡以后，我逐渐习惯了外面的规则，可每到喜悦之余，仍忍不住想邀人来共舞一曲，就像现在。
t_en: After leaving home I gradually grew used to the outside world with all its rules, but every time I feel happy I can't help but want to invite everyone to come and dance with me — just like now.

t_ch: 可惜你还只是个孩子，没法跟上这历练的舞步。
t_en: It's a real shame that you're just a kid and can't keep up with the pace of life's dance.

t_ch: 有什么好事吗？
t_en: Good news, I take it?

t_ch: 好事？当然，自从来了匹诺康尼，我的心灵已经许久没有感受到这样的舒畅。
t_en: Good news? Sure. Since coming to Penacony, my soul has rarely felt so at ease.

t_ch: 很快就有一笔大生意要谈成，或许是好几笔，金额大到可以买下一打小行星。
t_en: Soon there'll be a big deal coming down the line — could be several deals. More than enough to buy a dozen asteroids.

t_ch: 趁着这股东风，未来找上门的订单只会越来越多，就像滚雪球。
t_en: If I capitalize on this momentum, more and more of these deals are gonna come a-knockin' and things will start to snowball.

t_ch: 看！这就是善意带来的好报。
t_en: Just look! That's my reward for being kind.

t_ch: 而现在，孩子，或许我也需要你的善意。
t_en: And right now, kid, I might just need a little of your kindness.

t_ch: 是需要帮忙吗？
t_en: Need a hand with something?

t_ch: 你可真是懂我，这活儿不会亏待你的。
t_en: You get it. I'll really make this worth your while.

t_ch: 谢谢，没空。
t_en: Thanks, but I'm busy.

t_ch: 放心吧，报酬方面绝对不会少你的。
t_en: Don't worry. You definitely won't be getting the short end of the stick in terms of payment here.

t_ch: 准备分我股份？
t_en: Are you cutting me in on some company stock?

t_ch: 虽然不至于，但也不会亏待你的。
t_en: It's not quite that, but I'll be sure to make it worth your while.

t_ch: 我方才想起来，在匹诺康尼有桩重要的生意还没谈成，最近老忙着基金会方面的事，把这茬给忘干净了，可这边的单子一时半会儿也抽不开身。
t_en: I just remembered an important deal in Penacony that hasn't been settled yet. I've been so busy with the foundation that I completely forgot — and I'm completely tied up with things here for the time being.

t_ch: 很简单的，只用把我在<color=#dbc291ff>影视乐园寄存的一批玩具</color>取出来，带到<color=#dbc291ff>「黄金的时刻」的约定交易点</color>就行了。
t_en: It's a cinch: Just grab the <color=#dbc291ff>batch of toys I stashed at the theme park</color>, and take them over to the <color=#dbc291ff>meeting spot at Golden Hour</color>. That's it.

t_ch: 这笔钱是你的辛苦费。
t_en: And this here is your hard-earned remuneration.

t_ch: 毕竟，如果善意得不到回报，再伟大的善举也无法持久，你说对吧？
t_en: I mean, even the greatest act of kindness would never last long without a little recompense, right?

t_ch: 把我在<color=#dbc291ff>影视乐园寄存的一批玩具</color>取出来，带到<color=#dbc291ff>「黄金的时刻」的约定交易点</color>就行了。时刻保持你的善意，孩子。
t_en: Just grab the <color=#dbc291ff>batch of toys I stashed at the theme park</color> and take them over to the <color=#dbc291ff>meeting spot at Golden Hour</color>. And always be kind, kid.

t_ch: 来聊聊么？
t_en: Got time to chat?

t_ch: 好啊，孩子，但愿我的话语能让你获得些许平静。
t_en: Sure thing, kid. Hope my words can bring you a little peace.

t_ch: 关于基金会…
t_en: About the foundation...

t_ch: 那是很久以前的事了。当时我的公司还没做到这么大，不过也算小有名气。
t_en: That was a long time ago. Back then, my company wasn't really up to much but it still had made a little name for itself.

t_ch: 有天，我突然收到封邮件，是我学生时代最好的朋友寄来的，我们已经快十年没见面了。我非常激动，高价从别人那儿买了当天的跨星系船票。
t_en: Then one day, I suddenly got some mail from my high school bestie. It'd been ten years since we'd seen each other. I was so excited that I forked out top dollar for a same-day ticket on an interplanetary transport then and there.

t_ch: 可当我跟着定位找到她时，看见的却是她的葬礼。我这才知道，她毕生都致力于为战区民众提供医疗援助，最终被战火波及而死。
t_en: But when I made it over to her location, all I saw was... her funeral. Only then did I learn about how she'd dedicated her whole life to supplying medical assistance for refugees in war zones — and in the end, those flames of war had caught up with her.

t_ch: 我看着她的棺椁，就想啊，有什么是我能做的。然后，就有了基金会。
t_en: Looking at her coffin there, I thought: What can I do about this? And that's where the foundation comes in.

t_ch: 关于你自己…
t_en: About yourself...

t_ch: 我嘛，就是个卖玩具的。如果你家里有小孩的话，没准儿也买过我们的产品，没啥技术含量。
t_en: Me? I'm just a toy trader. If you have kids back home, you might have bought one of our products. They're not complex at all.

t_ch: 让孩子们都拥有一个快乐的童年，也是世界和平的一部分。
t_en: Giving children a happy childhood is one small part of world peace.

t_ch: 可能是因为基金会的缘故，大家都很支持我们的事业，不仅没有倒闭，还收购了不少小微企业，未来或许会变成银河系最大的玩具公司也说不定。
t_en: Maybe it's because of the foundation, but everyone has always been very supportive of our business. We have not just avoided bankruptcy — we've even managed to acquire a few other small businesses and might just be on our way to becoming the biggest toy manufacturer in the whole galaxy.

t_ch: 你看，这就是善意带来的善果。
t_en: Just look! That's my karma for being kind.

t_ch: 没什么想聊的了。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 你再三确认，眼前的家伙就是玩具的买主，他鬼鬼祟祟地站在这里，不知道还以为在交易见不得人的东西。
t_en: You confirm and re-confirm that this guy is indeed the buyer of the toys. He looks super-shady as he's standing there, as though he's planning to deal in something dodgy.

t_ch: 我要的货，带来了吗？
t_en: Did you bring the stuff?

t_ch: 买个玩具怎么跟做贼似的？
t_en: Why do you sound like a crook when we're just dealing toys?

t_ch: 玩具？——嗐，我这不是想塑造点氛围感嘛。
t_en: Toys? ... Hey, I'm just trying to create a little atmosphere here, right?

t_ch: 全匹诺康尼最正点的货。
t_en: The finest goods in all Penacony.

t_ch: 那是，物美价廉，放眼整个银河系也没有比这更好的。
t_en: They sure are. High quality at a low price. There's none better in all the galaxy.

t_ch: 千等万等总算等到了。一直没消息，我担心奥科萨娜女士中途反悔，现在看来，还是我以小人之心度君子之腹。
t_en: I'd been holding out for ages, but the wait is finally over. After so long with no word, I was worried that Miss Oksana got cold feet. But I've just been projecting my own stingy concerns onto a gracious lady.

t_ch: 有这批「玩具」，应该能把最艰难的半年撑过去了。
t_en: With these "toys," we should be good to make it through the rest of this difficult year coming up.

t_ch: 以这么低的价格卖给我们，多半还倒贴了不少进去。大恩大德，真是无以为报。
t_en: She must be taking a hit, letting us have the "toys" for such a low price. There really is no way to repay such magnanimous kindness.

t_ch: 玩具有这么重要?
t_en: Are toys really that important to you?

t_ch: 朋友，既然奥科萨娜女士让你来送货，想必十分信任你，我就不藏着掖着了。
t_en: My friend, if Miss Oksana entrusted you with the delivery, then she must place great faith in you. I won't keep you in the dark.

t_ch: 她不愿告诉你真相，恐怕是不希望做的好事被太多人知道吧。但我不这么想，好人就应该得到赞扬，善举就应当接受美誉。
t_en: She probably didn't tell you because she doesn't want too many people knowing about her charity. But I'm not having that: Good people should be praised, and good deeds should be lauded.

t_ch: 我的家乡在一个战火横飞的星域，军政府和傀儡政权下的政变就像星辰的闪烁那样常见。可无论当权者是谁，蒙受苦难和压迫的永远是人民。
t_en: My home system is in a segmentum racked by war. Military coups and puppet regimes are about as common as the stars in the sky. But whoever is in power, it's always the people who suffer under oppression.

t_ch: 即使榨干我们的最后一滴血，那些肉食者也会将剩余的尸体啃食殆尽，连食腐的鸟儿都会饿死。
t_en: Even if they squeeze out our last drop of blood, those savages will still find a way to pick all the meat off our bones so that even the vultures end up starving to death.

t_ch: 而为我们这些尸骨带来希望的，就是奥科萨娜女士。这位星系活动家通过基金会的影响力和财力，为我们提供了近乎免费的武装支持。
t_en: And who should bring hope to us corpses-to-be? Miss Oksana. Using her influence and finances at the foundation, this galactic activist provides us with armed support almost free of charge.

t_ch: 你眼前普普通通的玩具，等带回现实世界，就会转化为特定的筹码，用于兑换反抗暴政的武装。
t_en: These everyday toys you see here are able to be converted into special chips once they get back to the real world, then exchanged for arms to resist our oppressors.

t_ch: 现在懂了吗？她卖给我们的不是玩具，是我们的命，我们的未来。
t_en: Do you get it now? She's not selling toys to us, but our lives — and our futures.

t_ch: 为这个，哪怕把头割下来送给她，我也在所不惜。
t_en: And for that, I wouldn't hesitate one second to cut out my own heart and give it to her.

t_ch: 再见，亲爱的朋友。我羡慕这里的和平，但我不属于这里，在银河彼岸，还有更多人民在等待解放，那里才是我该回去的地方。
t_en: So long, dear friend. I envy the peace here, but Penacony's not where I belong. There's a whole load of people on the other side of the galaxy awaiting liberation, and that's where I need to be.

t_ch: 等你见到奥科萨娜女士，请务必向她传达我的感谢。朋友，愿你也能永远心存善意。
t_en: Next time you see Miss Oksana, please make sure you pass on my thanks to her. My friend, I hope you also keep your heart in the right place.

t_ch: 这就是新上市的型号？力场薄得像张纸。
t_en: Is this the latest model on the market? This force field is paper-thin.

t_ch: 已经是巷道战场能用的最大型号了，朋友，力场也是要配发生装置的。
t_en: It's the largest model out that can fit inside alleys and tunnels, friend — and force fields always need a generator.

t_ch: 想要大号的也行，每台机子后面拖个比本体还大的尾巴，给人当活靶子，如何？
t_en: We can always try something bigger: Like installing a clunky tail behind each generator to serve as target practice for the opposition. How about that?

t_ch: 当然，你们也可以去找星际和平公司和博识学会，只要他们肯卖。
t_en: Of course, you could also approach the IPC or the Intelligentsia Guild — if they're willing to sell.

t_ch: 话别这么说，你的「玩具」质量，这么多年大家也是有目共睹。只是那几个星域最近一个二个都不老实，经费也经不起折腾了。
t_en: Don't be like that. Anyone with eyes can tell how good the quality of your "toys" has been for years now. It's just that in those segmenta, none of them are honest anymore. Our finances just can't take the hit anymore.

t_ch: 到底有没有钱，我心里清楚得很，别玩这种砍价的把戏，一口价。
t_en: I know exactly how much you're able to afford, so quit the haggling. It's a fixed price.

t_ch: 不愿意也行，说不定有人比你们更需要这些「玩具」，我可以卖给他们。
t_en: If you don't want them, fine — there's probably someone out there who needs these "toys" even more than you do. I can always sell to them instead.

t_ch: 货我要了，定金会在明天打过来，别耍心眼。
t_en: I want the stuff. The down payment will be with you tomorrow... You don't have to be so cut-throat about it.

t_ch: 这才是聪明人。
t_en: That's a clever choice.

t_ch: 博识学会真该好好把你的大脑剖开，研究研究。到底是什么构造，才能活到现在没被噩梦吓死。
t_en: The Intelligentsia Guild really should dissect your brain for research and figure out how its construction is stopping you from being destroyed by nightmares.

t_ch: 神秘人头也不回地离开了，只留下奥科萨娜在原地。
t_en: The mysterious person departs without bothering to look back, leaving Oksana where they stood.

t_ch: 这么快？孩子，基金会里那些磨磨蹭蹭的办事员真该跟你学学。
t_en: That was quick. Those dithering schmucks at the foundation could really learn a thing or two from you, kid.

t_ch: 怎样？交易还顺利吗？
t_en: How'd the deal go? Everything smooth?

t_ch: 你是星系活动家的事…
t_en: About you being this galactic activist...

t_ch: …为什么要瞒着我？
t_en: ... Why did you hide everything from me?

t_ch: 我不是故意隐瞒的，你知道我的性格。我是不希望你戴着有色眼镜看我，哪怕那色彩带有正面意义。
t_en: It wasn't on purpose, as you know what kind of person I am. I just didn't want you to look at me with tinted glasses, even if they were tinted with positivity.

t_ch: 孩子，基金会出资援助他们，并非我一个人的功劳，银河里所有热爱和平和生命的人，他们的善意才是值得感激的。
t_en: Kid, the funding that the foundation invests isn't just coming from me. Champions of peace and life across the galaxy all deserve our gratitude for their kindness.

t_ch: 而我更不是什么伟大的星系活动家，只是一个为大家提供平台的领路人罢了。
t_en: And I'm not some great galactic activist — I'm just a guide who provides a platform for everyone to use.

t_ch: 宇宙中有很多这样的世界，在暴政和侵略的压迫下，连小小的生存都成奢望。在压迫者看来，他们根本就不是人，而是印钞机运转的燃料。
t_en: There are so many worlds out there in the universe under the yoke of tyranny and oppression where even mere survival has become a luxury. To their oppressors, the residents aren't even people, but simply fuel for their tyrants' money-printers.

t_ch: 看着他们的惨状，无论是谁都会同情，基金会就是在这样的基础上建立的。
t_en: Anybody would sympathize with them when confronted with such tragedy, and that forms the basis for why our foundation was established.

t_ch: 所以，我资助他们，单纯是出于个人的恻隐之心。对我而言，帮助他们反抗压迫并非是施舍，而是作为财富拥有者的一种义务和责任。
t_en: So I support them purely out of personal compassion. In my view, helping them fight against oppression isn't any kind of charity but rather my duty and responsibility as an individual of wealth.

t_ch: 善意，对吧？
t_en: Out of kindness, right?

t_ch: 没错，只要心怀善意，我们能做到的事，就连星神也无法企及。
t_en: Sure. So long as we have kind intentions, we can do things that not even the Aeons could dream of doing.

t_ch: 刚才那人是谁？
t_en: So who was that just then?

t_ch: 一脾气不太好的老客人，他们定制武器来镇——额，对抗反物质军团的入侵。
t_en: Just a regular of mine with a filthy temper. They make custom weapons to put down — uh, I mean to fight against the Antimatter Legion.

t_ch: 这次你做得很好，想想，因你的帮助，数亿的受压迫者将会推倒军政府的暴政。
t_en: You did great this time. Just think about it — thanks to your help, millions of oppressed people will be able to overthrow their tyrannical regime.

t_ch: 为此感到自豪吧，孩子。
t_en: You can be proud of that, kid.

t_ch: 虽然奥科萨娜说得义正言辞…但她和神秘人之间的可疑对话，还有种种不自然的表现，仍让你觉得蹊跷。
t_en: Despite Oksana speaking words of righteousness... her suspicious conversation with the mysterious person and other forced interactions leaves you feeling paranoid.

t_ch: （那个神秘人应该还没有走远…或许从她那里能打听到些什么。）
t_en: (That mysterious person shouldn't have wandered off too far... maybe you can ask her a few questions?)

t_ch: 你是刚才来见奥科萨娜的那家伙？怎么，那女人突然良心发现，打算给我们打个对折？
t_en: Are you the one who was coming to see Oksana before? What, don't tell me her conscience got the better of her, and she'd like to cut us a half-price deal now?

t_ch: 呵，以她的为人，这种事恐怕比公司改信阿哈的可能性还低。
t_en: Ha! Knowing her, there's more chance of the IPC converting to Aha than there is of that ever happening.

t_ch: 她不是个星系活动家吗？
t_en: Isn't she a galactic activist though?

t_ch: 活动家？天哪！哈哈哈哈哈，这是我这辈子听到的最好笑的笑话。
t_en: Activist? Oh, my! Hahaha... well that's the funniest thing I've ever heard in my life.

t_ch: 让我猜猜她是怎么和你说的，「啊！自从我儿时的好友因慈善事业而死，我便继承了她的衣钵，立志要抚平战争的伤痛，为此我们建立了基金会。」
t_en: Let me guess what she told you: "Oh, since my childhood friend died in the name of charity, I've taken up her mantle and dedicated myself to relieving the torments of war. And that's why we decided to establish this foundation of ours."

t_ch: 猜猜这套说辞在演讲会上说了多少次？又骗到了多少投资？
t_en: Take a guess how many times she's given this spiel before? And how much investment she's swindled out of people?

t_ch: 可是基金会…
t_en: But, what about the foundation...

t_ch: 知道基金会建立的初衷是什么吗？是洗钱！然后把钱投到军火的代工生产线上，基金会还能掩人耳目，多么精明的算盘。
t_en: You wanna know the real reason why the foundation was established? Money laundering! They take that money and invest it into weapons manufacturers, then use the foundation to pull the wool over people's eyes. It's a shrewd hustle.

t_ch: 银河闻名的大慈善家，我都快感动得落泪了。每当账户上的数字叮当作响，一船船的军火就扬帆远航。
t_en: A bigshot philanthropist throughout the galaxy? Don't make me sick. Every time there's another ding-dong on that bank account, a new shipment of arms sets sail.

t_ch: 知道整个故事里我最喜欢哪段吗？每当战争打到中场休息的时候，基金会的「人道主义救援物资」就会和大批的摄影师一起降落到地面。
t_en: Wanna know which part of the story I like best? Whenever any conflict reaches a ceasefire, the foundation's "humanitarian relief supplies" will be dispatched to the planet alongside a small army of photographers.

t_ch: 然后，他们会说：「请停止战争，大家坐下来和平地谈谈吧」。要我说，最适合她的工作也不是什么军火商，她就该去做个喜剧演员，和小丑们混在一起。
t_en: And then they'll say: "Please, put down arms and let everybody negotiate peacefully." I'm telling you, the best job for her isn't being an arms trader but rather a stand-up comedian. Just a bunch of clowns.

t_ch: 那句话怎么说来着？「保持善意，孩子」。
t_en: What was that line she just pulled? "Keep on being kind, kid."

t_ch: 不过和你说这么多也没用，我还得赶回去应付我们的总统大人。那长了俩脑袋的人形蛆虫，等我镇压完那群刁民，就带兵把他也给毙了。
t_en: But there's no use telling you all this. I have to hurry back to deal with that president of ours. Once I finish putting down the uprising, my forces will be executing that two-faced maggot too.

t_ch: 她正在两头下注。
t_en: She was hedging her bets.

t_ch: 她也把武器卖给了对面。
t_en: She was selling to the other side, too.

t_ch: 她把军火卖给你们两方，目的就是挑动战争，两头通吃。你们就甘心这么遂她的愿？
t_en: She was selling weapons to both of you to try and provoke a war so she could milk both sides. Are you happy for her to get away with it?

t_ch: 小家伙，说实话，见的烂人多了，我对你还挺有好感。可是啊，我要提醒你，宇宙并不如你想的那样纯粹。
t_en: Listen, buddy, to be honest with you, I've met a lot of crooks in my time, so I kind of like you. But let me remind you that this universe isn't as innocent as you think it is.

t_ch: 低价卖给匪徒们军火的是谁，我当然知道。只要他们还在反抗，我们就不得不继续从那女人手里收购军火。这么好的摇钱树，她怎么可能放手？
t_en: Of course, I know who cut those crooks a discount on those weapons. And we'll have to keep buying weapons from that lady for as long as they resist us. Why would she ever give up a cash cow like that?

t_ch: 就这，那些蒙在鼓里的暴民还得谢谢她呢！
t_en: Those ignorant thugs should be thanking her for all this!

t_ch: 军火商都是群两头下注的鬣狗，伤亡越多钱袋越鼓。但我们不在乎！我们只在乎比暴民更好的武器装备，只在乎能不能赢。
t_en: Arms dealers are like hyenas hedging their bets — they make money the more casualties there are. But we don't care! All we care about is having better weapons and better gear than those scoundrels, and whether or not we can beat them.

t_ch: 你是个和平世界的人，这些知识或许你一辈子也用不着。但谁能说清呢？
t_en: You come from a peaceful homeworld, and might not put this knowledge into practice for the rest of your life. But who knows?

t_ch: 买走玩具的小孩并不在意把玩具递给他们的双手是否沾了血。直到在未来的某天，他们自己也会变成红色的染料。
t_en: The kid who buys a toy doesn't care if the hands that sell it to him are covered in blood. Until one day in the future when they themselves are reduced to a red smear.

t_ch: 五个琥珀纪前，我们的星域也是个和平世界，看看现在，那地方比虚空鲸的胃袋还要恶臭。战争就是这样，你不知道它什么时候就落到自己头上。
t_en: Five Amber Eras ago, our segmentum used to be peaceful, too. But look at it now: It stinks worse than the stomach of a void song whale. That's war for you — you just never know when it'll fall on your head.

t_ch: 然后，军火商，雇佣兵，大家闻着味儿就蜂拥而来。利润的味道太甜了，品尝过一次，就再也没法戒掉这份依赖。
t_en: Then the swarms of arms dealers and mercenaries will come, flocking to the scent. The smell of profit is so sweet that with one whiff, you'll never be able to wean yourself off it.

t_ch: 再见，小家伙，希望未来不会在银河尽头的战场上见到你。
t_en: So long, buddy. I hope we never have to meet again on the battlefield at the end of the cosmos.

t_ch: 孩子，你怎么又回来了？有好事发生？是善意的回报到了吧。
t_en: Kid, what are you doing back here? Is it good news? It must be karma repaying your kindness.

t_ch: 你骗了我。
t_en: You lied to me.

t_ch: 是遇到什么烦心事了吗？快过来，让大姐姐来为你排忧解难。
t_en: What happened? Something bad? There there, let me take care of your worries.

t_ch: 生活是这样，总是会有不顺心的事发生，可我们还是得继续努力下去。如果连这点挫折都经受不住，那些还生活在战争里的人又该怎么办？
t_en: That's just life. There'll always be something annoying happening, but we have to keep soldiering on. If even we can't endure such setbacks, what hope do those living in actual war zones have?

t_ch: 我知道你军火商的身份了。
t_en: I know that you're an arms dealer.

t_ch: 啧…真该把那军头的嘴巴给缝上再放他走的。所以我才不喜欢和这些脑子里只装了反物质地雷的家伙打交道，政客们可比他机灵多了。
t_en: Tsk... I should have sewn that general's lips shut before I let him go. That's why I hate having to deal with these guys who only have anti-matter mines for brains. Politicians are far smarter.

t_ch: 我都知道了。
t_en: I know everything.

t_ch: 哎呀，不会又是哪里的反抗组织给我寄了感谢信吧？
t_en: Yikes. Don't tell me there's another rebel group who's sent me a thank you letter?

t_ch: 说真的，基金会做事已经尽量低调了。但每次就是有些好心人，不顾生命危险跑去战场，把我们的援助活动给拍下来。
t_en: To tell you the truth, we try to keep things as low profile as we can at the foundation. But every time there'll be some kind-hearted people who risk their necks out there on the battlefield taking photos of our aid.

t_ch: 不是你雇去的？
t_en: They're not hired by you?

t_ch: 不是我说，咱俩萍水相逢一场，何必这么针锋相对？
t_en: Didn't I already say? Ours was a chance rendezvous, so why should we be at loggerheads?

t_ch: 我出钱，你办事，这就是世界运行的底层逻辑。他们拿到了想要的武器，我拿到了钱，你拿到了报酬，何乐而不为呢。
t_en: I pay the money, and you do the job. That's the underlying logic behind how the whole world works. They got the weapons they wanted. I got my money. And you got your payment, too. What's wrong with having it good for a change?

t_ch: 演，接着演。
t_en: Games within games.

t_ch: 你不会是听了谁诽谤基金会的话吧？这时代是这样，大家都爱把人推上神坛，再把他打倒。
t_en: Don't tell me you believe that slander against the foundation? That's just the way it is, these days — everyone loves to hold up the tall poppy before cutting it down.

t_ch: 侮辱我个人可以，但如果有谁对大家的劳动成果指手画脚，我绝不允许。
t_en: It's fine if you want to insult me. But I will never stand for people criticizing everyone else's hard work.

t_ch: 煽动战争也是慈善的一部分？
t_en: Is inciting war part of your charity?

t_ch: 啧…看来你都知道了啊。
t_en: Tsk... Looks like you've figured everything out.

t_ch: 要我说，大人的事，小朋友就别这么上心——晚上会睡不着的。
t_en: If you ask me, kids shouldn't work themselves up about grown-up things... or they won't be able to sleep at night.

t_ch: 再者说，也不是我煽动的战争，他们先有的需求，我再点的火。我只是遵守了作为商人的基本素养，给客户提供他们所需的商品而已。
t_en: And besides, I was never the instigator. They came to me with their request, and I just helped them light the fuse. All I'm doing is following basic business etiquette by giving customers the products they need.

t_ch: 孩子，梦境外的世界哪来你这种邪啊正的，有钱赚、有命花才是唯一的真理。
t_en: Kid, there's no such thing as right and wrong in the world outside the Dreamscape. Having money, and staying alive so you can spend the money — that's the only truth there is.

t_ch: 假如把你放到我这个位置，你会怎么做？那可是真金白银的钱，如果把银行里的数字换成纸钞，可以铺满一座山，不，铺满一片山脉。
t_en: If you were in my shoes, what would you do? That's cold hard cash we're talking about. If I pulled it all out of the bank, the stacks would cover up a mountain — no, make that a whole mountain range.

t_ch: 只要反抗军还有力气闹腾，军政府的需求就会变成一个无底洞，把从民众身上抢来的钱原封不动地送给我们。
t_en: As long as the rebels have enough steam to resist, then the junta's weapon orders represent a bottomless pit — funneling money stolen from the people directly to us.

t_ch: 所以，反抗军不能赢，但也不能输。这就应了那句老话，叫「养寇自重」。哦不，是「放长线钓大鱼」。
t_en: Therefore, the rebels cannot win — but they can't lose, either. It's just like that old fable about the frog carrying the scorpion across the river... Ah, no — it's "casting a long line to catch a bigger fish."

t_ch: 别用那种指责的眼神看我。要知道，没我的装备支持，那群人连反抗都做不到，军方的镇压机器可以在一天内把整个片区连地基拔起来。
t_en: Don't give me that snide look. I'll have you know that without my supplies, they wouldn't even get the chance to resist. The state's machine of oppression would just eradicate them before nightfall, roots and all.

t_ch: 更何况，我还让基金会用这些钱去援助战区的民众，四舍五入，我不是帮老百姓把钱给抢回来了嘛。
t_en: And what's more, I'm always asking the foundation to put this money back into aid to help war refugees. If you round everything up, am I not actually helping the people get their own money back?

t_ch: 你不是要消灭战争吗？
t_en: Didn't you say you want to eliminate war?

t_ch: 我从战争中学到的唯一知识，就是战争从未改变。孩子，既然你没法阻止它，那就只能让它为你所用。哪里有交火，哪里就有盈利。
t_en: The only lesson I learned from war is that nothing ever changes. Kid, if you can't stop it, then you might as well try and put it to work for you. Wherever there's warfare, there are profits to be made.

t_ch: 也不知道家族给我灌了什么迷魂药，居然放着大好的生意不做，天天拉人来搞慈善。
t_en: I don't know what The Family dosed me with, spurning such a great line of business like this and recruiting people into the charity cause instead.

t_ch: 这笔钱，就当是我骗过你的补偿，从此我们互不相识，你没见过我，我也没见过你。
t_en: This sum should make up for me deceiving you. From now on, we don't know each other. You've never seen me and I've never seen you.

t_ch: 收回你的臭钱。
t_en: You can keep your filthy blood money.

t_ch: 你再好好想想？哪怕在匹诺康尼，每天也有无数人为了这些钱整日疲于奔命。
t_en: Care to reconsider? Even here in Penacony you can find countless people struggling day and night to try and make this kind of money.

t_ch: 丢掉你那正义必胜的小孩子气吧。远在天边的几个数字和近在眼前的利益，正确选项很明确了——钱不够我可以再加。
t_en: Ditch your naive ideas about justice always prevailing. One is an abstract number light-years away, and the other is profits right under your nose — the right answer couldn't be any clearer. If it helps, I can give you even more.

t_ch: 我忍不了了！
t_en: I can't take it any more!

t_ch: 呔！你这战争贩子，先吃俺开拓者一棒！
t_en: Listen up, warmonger! I'll give you a taste of some Trailblazer medicine!

t_ch: 你或许在期待一场热血澎湃的战斗，可惜，我们的奥科萨娜女士既不热血也不澎湃。
t_en: You may be anticipating a heated battle, but unfortunately, Miss Oksana offers you neither heat nor battle.

t_ch: 直到猎犬的众人赶到现场，这位闻名遐迩的商界大亨还没能缓过神来。
t_en: The famous entrepreneur was still struggling to recover when the Bloodhounds have made it onto the scene.

t_ch: 我也正有此意。
t_en: That's what I meant.

t_ch: 这就对了，那些人的生死和我们又有何干系，有钱赚才是硬道理。
t_en: That's right. What's it to us whether those people live or die? Making money is the be-all and end-all.

t_ch: 我奥科萨娜最喜欢你这种识相的孩子。
t_en: Sensible kids like you are my favorite.

t_ch: 你这手下得真狠，有没有兴趣加入我们猎犬？待遇好商量。
t_en: Absolutely ruthless. Ever thought about joining up with us Bloodhounds? We can negotiate on your salary.

t_ch: 不开玩笑了，要不是你及时抓住了这家伙，银河里不知道又有多少星球要生灵涂炭。
t_en: I'm not even kidding: If it weren't for you catching this crook so soon, who knows how many planets in the cosmos would have been obliterated?

t_ch: 这是你的奖金，那些因你而活下来的孩子，我替他们感谢你。
t_en: Here's your bonus. And on behalf of all those children who are still alive because of you: Thank you.

t_ch: 乖乖跟我们走吧，「大慈善家」？
t_en: Are you gonna come quietly, "bigshot philanthropist"?

t_ch: 总有一天，你会为自己的天真后悔的。
t_en: One day, you will come to regret your naivety.

t_ch: 奥科萨娜恶狠狠地盯着你，看来，她也终于感受到了——善意的回报。
t_en: Oksana stares at you in rage. Looks like she's finally getting a taste of her own karma.

t_ch: （从奥科萨娜手里拿到了封口费，可左想右想还是咽不下这口气。）
t_en: (You take the hush money from Oksana, but no matter which way you think about it, you can't quite stomach it.)

t_ch: （万一让她从这里离开，不知道又有多少地方会被战火掩埋。）
t_en: (If by chance she were to leave here, who knows how many planets would be engulfed in the flames of war.)

t_ch: 在战争爆发前，或许你可以<color=#dbc291ff>向伍尔西求助</color>。
t_en: Before war breaks out, perhaps you can <color=#dbc291ff>ask Woolsey for help</color>.

t_ch: 如果现在离开…
t_en: If you leave now, then...

t_ch: 您好，需要帮助吗？
t_en: Hello. Need any help?

t_ch: 我要举报。
t_en: I'd like to make a report.

t_ch: 一个叫奥科萨娜的军火商即将引发一场星系战争。
t_en: An arms dealer named Oksana is about to set off an intergalactic war.

t_ch: 什么？那个战争贩子是怎么找回情绪的？这可是紧急事故，快带我们过去。
t_en: What? How did that warmonger find her emotions again? This is an emergency. Take us to her now.

t_ch: 跑得还挺快，但凡放在其他星球，没准儿就真给她溜了。
t_en: She's pretty fast, but if it were on any other planet, she really might just get away.

t_ch: 别担心小家伙，她可逃不出猎犬的鼻子。
t_en: Don't worry, buddy. She can't escape from the nose of a Bloodhound.

t_ch: 因为有你的消息，我们封锁了整个白日梦酒店，除非她能在房间里凭空搓出一台超光速引擎。否则，就算是反物质军团来了也救不了她。
t_en: Thanks to your intel, we were able to cordon off the entirety of The Reverie. Unless she can somehow put together an FTL drive in her room out of thin air, not even the Antimatter Legion would be able to come and rescue her.

t_ch: 这是你的奖金，是你让无数星球免于战乱，我替孩子们感谢你的善意。
t_en: Here's your reward. You have saved countless planets from the ravages of war. On behalf of all those affected children: Thank you.

t_ch: 再见。
t_en: Bye.

t_ch: 再见，孩子，希望我们能早日在那个和平的世界相遇。
t_en: So long, kid. Hope we soon get that chance to meet up in that peaceful world.

t_ch: 你偷偷看了眼，精致的包装里都是很普通的玩具，你无法理解它们为何如此畅销。或许这就是奥科萨娜所说的，「善意」的回报吧。
t_en: Taking a peek into the immaculate bag, you see it's filled with ordinary toys. You cannot understand how they're selling so well. Perhaps this is what Oksana might call a return of "goodwill."

t_ch: …可恶…那家伙一定作弊了，我必须将{F#她}{M#他}的卑鄙手段揭露出来才行……
t_en: ...Blast... That person must've cheated. I must expose {F#her}{M#his} despicable methods...

t_ch: 谁作弊了？
t_en: Who cheated?

t_ch: 什么手段？
t_en: What methods?

t_ch: 关你什么事，都说了我不接受采访…是、是你？！
t_en: Mind your own business. I already said I don't take interviews... I—It's you!?

t_ch: 我们认识吗？
t_en: Do we know each other?

t_ch: 你大概不认识我，但你这张脸，就算化成灰我都能认出来！
t_en: You probably don't, but that face of yours, I'd recognize it anywhere!

t_ch: 你是我的粉丝？
t_en: You're a fan of mine?

t_ch: 就你？你配吗！你以为自己是知更鸟小姐吗？
t_en: You? Don't flatter yourself! What, you think you're like Miss Robin?

t_ch: 废话少说，你在盛会海选的比赛中作弊了吧，是不是偷偷买通评委了——我告诉你，别想狡辩，你的所作所为我可都看在眼里！
t_en: Stop this nonsense. You cheated at the festivity auditions, didn't you? Did you bribe the panel? I'll warn you to stop denying it. I've got my eye on every move you make!

t_ch: 我奉劝你赶紧把冠军还给应得的选手，然后公开承认自己的错误，不然我就不客气了！
t_en: You'd better hurry up and return the winner's title to the rightful contestant, then publicly confess your crime, or you'll be sorry!

t_ch: 真的吗，我好怕哦。
t_en: Really? Oh goodness gracious, I'm so scared.

t_ch: 你！真是死不悔改，你会为此后悔的！
t_en: You! Churlish, recalcitrant! You'll regret this!

t_ch: 你什么冠军？
t_en: You're the winner of what again?

t_ch: 你！你居然还敢拿偷来的冠军嘲讽我，我饶不了你！
t_en: You! How dare you mock me with a stolen crown! I'll end you!

t_ch: 要来瓶美梦糖浆吗？
t_en: How about a bottle of Dream Syrup?

t_ch: 你！你才脑子有问题，我清醒得很！
t_en: You! You've got a screw loose! I'm fully sober!

t_ch: 多说无益，对付你这种作弊者，就应该给你点皮肉之苦，看招吧！
t_en: Words are pointless. Cheaters like you ought to be taught a lesson in pain! Get ready!

t_ch: 别、别得意，我今天刚好状态不佳，才让你钻了空子。
t_en: D—Don't get full of yourself. I was not at my peak today, so you managed to sneak through my defenses.

t_ch: 走着瞧吧，我早晚要你好看！
t_en: We'll see about this! You're going to be sorry one of these days!

t_ch: …算你有点本事，我们走着瞧！
t_en: ...You've got some skill, I'll give you that. You watch your back!

t_ch: …你、你刚刚对我做了什么？不是忆庭的手段，也不像机器头信徒惯用的那一套…你究竟是什么人？
t_en: ...Wh—What did you do to me? This isn't one of the Garden's tricks, nor does it look like any move used by Droidhead disciples... Who the heck are you?

t_ch: 你想问我就一定要答？
t_en: Do I have to answer your question?

t_ch: 我凭什么告诉你？
t_en: Why should I tell you?

t_ch: 你！学人说话好玩吗！不想说就算了，我也懒得知道。不过…这么看来你确实有些本事……
t_en: You! You think it's funny to imitate me! Who cares if you don't want to tell me. I didn't even want to know anyway. But... it appears you've got some moves on you after all...

t_ch: 这样吧，我们来打个赌，你如果能破解我出的谜题，我就不再纠缠你，否则你就得当众宣布自己是通过一些见不得光的手段赢得了冠军，然后对获得第二名的选手郑重道歉。
t_en: How about this? Let's make a wager: If you can solve my riddle, I'll leave you alone. Otherwise, you'll announce that you won the title through unsavory means, and then properly apologize to the contestant who came in second.

t_ch: 先别急着拒绝，你也不想之后一直得费心费神防备着我，对吧？
t_en: Don't be in a rush to refuse — you don't want to have to keep watching your back from now on, do you?

t_ch: 你怎么不向我道歉？
t_en: Why don't you apologize to me then?

t_ch: …好吧，如果你赢了，我不止不再针对你，还会当众向你道歉，行了吧！
t_en: ...Fine. If you win, I'll stop pursuing you. And I'll even apologize to you in front of everyone. How's that!?

t_ch: 我不想陪你浪费时间。
t_en: You're wasting my time.

t_ch: 哼，果然是个胆小鬼，就知道你不敢——但这可由不得你！
t_en: Hah, so you're a coward after all. I knew you wouldn't dare, but it's not up to you!

t_ch: 看到这个梦境迷钟了吗？修好它，就算你赢了。
t_en: See this Dream Ticker? Repair it, and you win.

t_ch: 看到这个梦境迷钟了吗？修好它，就算你赢了。
t_en: See this Dream Ticker? Repair it, and you win.

t_ch: 这不可能！你怎么、怎么…一定是误打误撞解开的…对、一定是！可恶，算你走运……
t_en: This is impossible! H—How did you... You must have fixed it with sheer luck... That must be it! Argh, you'd better thank your lucky stars...

t_ch: 行吧，我这人说话算话，以后不会再对你做什么，你可以安心了。
t_en: Fine. I'll uphold my end of the wager: I won't pursue you again, and you can count on that.

t_ch: 你是不是忘了什么？
t_en: You forgetting something else?

t_ch: …我…我当然没忘！是…是我错了，你大人有大量，就原谅我吧……
t_en: ...I ...Of course I haven't! I... I was wrong. Please forgive me...

t_ch: 你说什么，我没听清。
t_en: What's that you said? I can't hear you.

t_ch: …我说，我错了，原谅我吧！
t_en: ...I said, I was wrong. Forgive me!

t_ch: 再大点声。
t_en: Louder.

t_ch: 你耍我呢？！还有完没完了，没事赶紧从我眼前消失，我可一点都不想再看到你了！
t_en: You screwing with me? Stop dragging this out and go away! I don't ever want to see you again!

t_ch: …你怎么还不走？我警告你啊，如果你敢在大庭广众之下，一时冲动做出什么蠢事来，明天的头条可就精彩了！
t_en: ...What are you still doing here? I'm warning you, if you dare show yourself in public again and do something rash, tomorrow's headlines will be a surefire hit!

t_ch: 「海选冠军心胸狭隘道德败坏、当街欺辱同期选手」——你也不想看到这样的新闻，对吧？
t_en: "Scornful Audition Champion of Questionable Morals Bullies Fellow Contestant in the Street." You wouldn't want something like that to appear in the news, would you?

t_ch: 另外…好心劝你一句，虽然你这次凭着些见不得光的小伎俩赢得了冠军，但也别太把自己当回事。
t_en: Also... here's a word of advice: You may have won through shameful trickery this time, but don't pat yourself on the back just yet.

t_ch: 就算我一时不查惜败于你，但像你这样的俗人，永远也无法理解我崇高的理想与追求！
t_en: Even if I was narrowly defeated by you, someone as uncouth as you would never understand the admirable ideals and pursuits of one such as I!

t_ch: ——单就这点而言，你已经输了，知道吗！
t_en: ...You've already lost just based on this singular trait. You hear me!

t_ch: 我完美的计划全都被打乱了，可恶——可恶啊啊啊！
t_en: My perfect plan has totally been disrupted, blast it... Blast it all!!!

t_ch: 如果不是那个讨厌的家伙横插一脚，这届冠军本该是我的囊中之物！
t_en: If it weren't for that meddling fool, this season's championship should have been in the bag! My bag!

t_ch: 明明没有人能拒绝镜子、没有人能拒绝自己心中的「完美」…碎片怎么就失效了呢……
t_en: No one is supposed to be able to resist the mirror, no one should be able to refuse the "perfection" in their own hearts... Why did the fragment not work...

t_ch: 难道是催眠？不、也不对…可恶，明明不过是个从未窥见过「完美」的庸俗之辈，{F#她}{M#他}那蛊惑人心的手段到底是怎么回事！
t_en: Could it have been hypnosis? No, not possible... Damn, it was obviously some boorish buffoon who'd never even glimpsed "perfection" before. What exactly did {F#she}{M#he} do to charm everyone!?

t_ch: 真是个难缠的家伙，看来我之后得更加小心才行……
t_en: What a troublesome fellow. It appears I'll have to be more cautious from now on...

t_ch: …你、你还想干什么？
t_en: ...Wh—What else do you want?

t_ch: 我告诉你，虽然我不会再针对你了，但也不代表我会任由你找麻烦。
t_en: I told you, I may not pursue you anymore, but it doesn't mean that I'll allow you to cause trouble.

t_ch: 你最好别打什么坏主意，不然猎犬的警卫可不是吃素的！
t_en: You'd better not get any funny ideas, or the Hounds will hunt you down!

t_ch: 麻烦你离我远点，咱们井水不犯河水，这样对彼此都好。
t_en: Please stay far away from me. Let's keep to ourselves. It's much better for everyone.

t_ch: 可这下好了，都怪那个小混蛋！
t_en: Bah, fantastic! It's all that brat's fault!

t_ch: 我不远万里赶来匹诺康尼，就是为了能够在谐乐大典上，为知更鸟小姐献上镜子的碎片。
t_en: I came such a long way to Penacony just to be able to give Miss Robin a mirror fragment at the Charmony Festival.

t_ch: 她是那么纯粹…她的歌声那么动人，她理当在万众瞩目之下加冕，成为「美」的代言人、成为「美」本身！
t_en: She's the epitome of purity... Her voice is so moving that she should be crowned before the eyes of millions, becoming the spokesperson and avatar of Beauty!

t_ch: 这场海选本应成为她脚下通向至高无上的红毯，这场盛典本应成为她从此膺获神性光辉的舞台，可现在…可现在——
t_en: This audition round should have paved the way for her ascent towards the glamorous red carpet. This festival should have been the glorious stage where she attains divinity, but now... now...

t_ch: 不、我还不能放弃，那些残砖碎瓦配不上她…那些愚者俗人，根本就不懂如何欣赏真正的「美」！
t_en: No, I refuse to give up. The rabble does not deserve her... Those fools and commoners, they do not even know what true beauty is!

t_ch: 我还不能放弃…无论如何，我都要见知更鸟小姐一面！
t_en: I can't give up yet... No matter what, I must see Miss Robin!

t_ch: 眼前的男人神色恍惚，面上泛红、嘴角噙笑，似乎陷入了什么美妙的幻觉。
t_en: The man's eyes are dazed, his face flushed, and the corners of his lips curled in a smile, as if he had been enchanted by some splendid illusion.

t_ch: 尊、尊敬的知更鸟小姐，能以这样的方式与您相见，我实在是三生有幸。
t_en: E—Esteemed Miss Robin, to be able to meet you in such a manner... I am truly blessed.

t_ch: 这么说来或许有些冒昧，但在见到您之前，我其实一直无法理解他们的狂热，无法理解那些人为何会为了祂奋不顾身……
t_en: It may be a little rude to say this, but before meeting you, I could not understand their fervor. Why they would throw themselves in harm's way for THEM...

t_ch: 直到我在您身上看见了真正的「美」，从您的歌声中听见了宇宙的井然与和谐。
t_en: ...until I saw true beauty emanating from you, that I heard the universe's order and harmony from your voice.

t_ch: 请允许我将自己的一切奉献给您，知更鸟小姐——
t_en: Please allow me to offer myself to you, Miss Robin...

t_ch: 我是您最忠实的仆从、最虔诚的信徒，您所指的方向将会是我前进唯一的道路！
t_en: I am your most loyal servant, your most devout follower. Wherever you command me, it shall be the only path I go!

t_ch: 请允许我将自己的一切奉献给您，知更鸟小姐——
t_en: Please allow me to offer myself to you, Miss Robin...

t_ch: 我是您最忠实的仆从、最虔诚的信徒，您所指的方向将会是我前进唯一的道路！
t_en: I am your most loyal servant, your most devout follower. Wherever you command me, it shall be the only path I go!

t_ch: 我有话要问你。
t_en: I have something to ask you.

t_ch: 你想问我就一定要答？算了吧，我和你没什么可说的。
t_en: What, and I have to answer? Forget it. We have nothing to say to each other.

t_ch: 你为什么这么针对我？
t_en: What have you got against me?

t_ch: 针对你？好笑，你都不先反省一下自己的所作所为吗？
t_en: Against you? You're kidding. Why don't you think about what you've done?

t_ch: 要是人人都像你一样，通过作弊来获胜，那些通过自己的努力进行比赛的选手又该怎么办？
t_en: If everyone cheated to get ahead like you, how is that fair to the contestants who poured both sweat and tears into their craft?

t_ch: 你还想怎么对付我？
t_en: What else do you intend to do to me?
